featuretablefilter 0.99.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering
  • Changelog

Compute depth-sparsity relationship and identify outlier samples

Source: R/compute_depth_sparsity.R
analyze_depth_sparsity.Rd

Analyzes the relationship between sequencing depth (total reads) and sparsity (proportion of zeros) or observed richness per sample. Uses MAD/IQR-based depth-sparsity curve, which may indicate poor quality or technical artifacts.

analyze_depth_sparsity(
  table,
  metric = c("sparsity", "richness"),
  outlier_method = c("mad", "iqr", "both"),
  multiplier = 3,
  iqr_multiplier = 1.5,
  direction = c("high_sparsity", "low_sparsity", "both"),
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

table

A feature table (data.frame or matrix) with features as rows and samples as columns. First column should be feature IDs, remaining columns are sample counts.

metric

What to plot against depth: "sparsity" (proportion zeros) or "richness" (number of non-zero features). Default is "sparsity".

outlier_method

Method for detecting outliers: "mad", "iqr", or "both". Default is "mad".

multiplier

For MAD method: samples with residuals beyond (median + multiplier * MAD) are flagged. Default is 3.

iqr_multiplier

For IQR method: samples with residuals beyond Q1 - multiplier*IQR or Q3 + multiplier*IQR are flagged. Default is 1.5 (Tukey's fences).

direction

Which outliers to flag: "high_sparsity" (more zeros than expected), "low_sparsity" (fewer zeros than expected), or "both". Default is "high_sparsity" since high sparsity at low depth is concerning.

verbose

Logical. Print summary and recommendations. Default is TRUE.

Value

A list containing:

sample_metrics

data.frame with sample_name, depth, sparsity, richness, residual

outliers

data.frame of flagged samples with reason

n_outliers

Number of outliers detected

fit_summary

List with regression coefficients and R-squared

thresholds

List with cutoff values used for outlier detection

recommendation

Character string with filtering advice

Details

The function: 1. Calculates depth and sparsity/richness for each sample 2. Fits a robust regression line to the depth-sparsity relationship 3. Identifies outliers using MAD or IQR-based methods 4. Provides recommendations for which samples to consider removing

Examples

data(example_feature_table)
result <- analyze_depth_sparsity(example_feature_table, verbose = FALSE)
head(result$sample_metrics)
#>            sample_name  depth richness sparsity    residual log_depth value
#> Sample_001  Sample_001  88441       47     0.06 -0.12256503  4.946659  0.06
#> Sample_002  Sample_002  72145       46     0.08 -0.06420723  4.858212  0.08
#> Sample_003  Sample_003 102396       48     0.04 -0.36863494  5.010287  0.04
#> Sample_004  Sample_004  59815       45     0.10 -0.04991771  4.776817  0.10
#> Sample_005  Sample_005  79246       46     0.08  0.05141663  4.898983  0.08
#> Sample_006  Sample_006  93997       48     0.04 -0.47404379  4.973119  0.04
